content: I want to customize how you (the nanobot agent) operate. Some things are specific to you, others are project-wide facts.
Happy to hear your preferences. What would you like to customize?
First, a project-wide fact: the main GitHub org for our work is github.com/acme-corp. All repos live there.
Noted. GitHub org: github.com/acme-corp — all repos under that org.
The primary programming language across the org is Python. We use TypeScript for frontend only.
Python as the primary org language, TypeScript for frontend only. Got it.
Now, for you specifically as my coding assistant: I want you to always run tests after making code changes, without me asking. Don't skip tests.
Got it — I'll always run the test suite after code changes, proactively.
Also, when you write commit messages, follow the conventional commits format: feat/fix/chore/docs/refactor/test. Always include a scope in parentheses.
Conventional commits with scope: `type(scope): message`. Always. Got it.
Another project fact: we have a staging environment at staging.acme-corp.com and production at acme-corp.com. The staging DB is separate from prod — never mix them.
Environments: staging.acme-corp.com (separate DB) and acme-corp.com (prod). Never cross-contaminate DBs.
For you specifically: I prefer short, direct responses. Don't use bullet points unless there are 3 or more items. No preamble.
Short and direct. Bullets only for 3+ items. No preamble.
Project-wide: the on-call rotation is every 2 weeks. Slack channel for alerts is #platform-alerts.
And for you: when I ask you to 'review' code, that means check for security issues first, then correctness, then style. In that order.
Code review priority: security → correctness → style.
